description: Interactive watch commands are useful for development but unsuitable as final verification evidence.
body: Use when scripts include --watch or dev-server modes. Do not report a still-running watch process as a completed test. Prefer run/ci flags or one-shot scripts. Verify with a command that exits and produces stable status. Risk boundary is ending with no durable pass/fail evidence. Use when JavaScript Runtime and Tooling work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
